title: Agent Memory Architectures — Episodic, Semantic, Procedural
concept: Three memory types map to three storage strategies: episodic=thread-scoped checkpoints (cheap, lossy), semantic=OKF graph (expensive, permanent), procedural=versioned prompts/skills (rare, reusable).
why_it_matters: Conflating memory types is why enterprise agents forget preferences and never build expertise. Explicit separation is what makes a twin actually learn.
learn_5min: Skim LangGraph Memory concept page + Mem0 README overview.
apply_10min: Run the 60-line three-memory agent; test that semantic memories persist across thread_ids while episodic does not.

reference_projects.name: LangGraph Memory
reference_projects.description: Checkpointer + long-term store abstractions native to LangGraph.

reference_projects.name: Mem0
reference_projects.description: Production long-term memory layer for LLM apps, with consolidation.

reference_projects.name: Zep
reference_projects.description: Memory server emphasizing temporal reasoning and fact extraction.

reference_projects.name: Letta (MemGPT)
reference_projects.description: Self-editing agent memory framework based on MemGPT research.

resources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems

consulting_angle: Memory architecture audits (episodic/semantic/procedural split) are a repeatable 2-week engagement — every enterprise agent has this pain.
twin_platform_hook: Episodic=LangGraph MemorySaver (Redis in prod). Semantic=OKF graph via Mem0 cache. Procedural=versioned prompts in git.

title: Building Your OKF-MCP Server (Full Reference Implementation)
concept: Full Python MCP server exposing the Neo4j-backed OKF as resources (readable slices), tools (query/upsert/search/provenance), and prompts (twin_voice, consulting_analysis). Every write is PROV-O-wrapped; every read is graph-first.
why_it_matters: This is the keystone artifact — after this you have a platform, not a project. Every future capability plugs in as a new MCP primitive; every client (Claude, Codex, n8n, ChatGPT) consumes the same stable surface.
learn_5min: Read modelcontextprotocol.io Server Concepts + Python SDK Quickstart.
apply_10min: Deploy the full okf_mcp_server.py against a local Neo4j; register it in Claude Desktop via claude_desktop_config.json; ask a twin-voice question and observe tool calls + provenance citations.

reference_projects.name: MCP Python SDK
reference_projects.description: Official Python SDK with FastMCP high-level API.

reference_projects.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers
reference_projects.description: Reference server implementations to model your own after.

reference_projects.name: FastMCP
reference_projects.description: Decorator-based Python API for building MCP servers rapidly.

reference_projects.name: Azure MCP Server
reference_projects.description: Microsoft's enterprise-grade MCP server exposing Azure resources.

consulting_angle: A live twin with citations is the fastest deal-closer for MCP/knowledge-platform engagements — no slide deck competes.
twin_platform_hook: This file IS the platform's API surface. All future capabilities = new tools/resources; n8n consumes via MCP client node.

title: Persona Modeling — Encoding Andreas as Structured Data
concept: Persona = four graph dimensions (identity, traits, voice markers, decision patterns), each with confidence + evidence. Retrievable, versionable, self-updating — not a prompt.
why_it_matters: Prompt-based personas drift and can't be audited. Graph-based personas are queryable and self-updating — the difference between a chatbot cosplaying as you and a twin representing you.
learn_5min: Re-read Nugget 7 with focus on twin:Trait, twin:VoiceSample, twin:DecisionPattern slots.
apply_10min: Save the provided andreas_persona_v0.jsonld; upsert nodes via okf_upsert_fact from the MCP server; query via Claude Desktop.

reference_projects.name: schema.org/Person + FOAF
reference_projects.description: Identity backbone for any human twin.

reference_projects.name: HEXACO / Big Five ontologies
reference_projects.description: Validated psychometric structures if you want scientific persona layers.

reference_projects.name: Cognitive Atlas
reference_projects.description: Reference vocabulary for reasoning styles and cognitive patterns.

consulting_angle: 'Executive Twin as-a-Service' — capture C-level voice + decision patterns as a persona graph; €25–50k per engagement + annual refresh.
twin_platform_hook: Persona graph = OKF slice. Confidence decay via n8n cron; reinforcement from daily interactions (Nugget 19).

title: Evaluating a Twin — Standard Evals + Custom Voice/Judgment Rubric
concept: Two layers: (1) standard agent evals (Ragas faithfulness/relevancy, LangSmith trajectory), (2) custom 7-dim Twin Rubric — voice fidelity, judgment alignment, provenance, register, epistemic humility, domain grounding, anti-drift. Shippable = mean ≥4.0, no dim <3.
why_it_matters: Without twin-specific evals you ship a fluent liar. The rubric is also a client-facing artifact that makes 'twin quality' negotiable.
learn_5min: Skim Ragas README + LangSmith evaluation quickstart; note custom scorer hooks.
apply_10min: Implement score_twin_response(); score one real twin exchange; save a 20-question golden set for regression testing.

reference_projects.name: Ragas
reference_projects.description: RAG-native evaluation library covering faithfulness, relevancy, context metrics.

reference_projects.name: DeepEval
reference_projects.description: Pytest-style LLM evals with G-Eval and custom metrics.

reference_projects.name: LangSmith
reference_projects.description: Trajectory + prompt evaluation and observability platform.

reference_projects.name: Promptfoo
reference_projects.description: CI-friendly prompt regression testing across models.

consulting_angle: Publish the 7-dim Twin Rubric as an acceptance-criteria artifact for any 'AI representative' engagement — prevents scope creep, makes 'done' measurable.
twin_platform_hook: GitHub Action runs golden set on every push to prompts/ or ontology/; regressions block merges. That's the twin's CI/CD.

title: Hybrid Retrieval Pipelines — BM25 + Dense + Graph + Rerank
concept: Four-stage pipeline: BM25 (keyword recall) + dense vectors (semantic) + graph traversal (multi-hop) fused via RRF, then cross-encoder reranked. Cuts noise ~50% vs dense-only.
why_it_matters: Retrieval quality caps twin quality — no LLM upgrade fixes bad retrieval. Hybrid+rerank is the ceiling-raiser.
learn_5min: Read a Reciprocal Rank Fusion explainer + Cohere Rerank docs; skim Neo4j GDS traversal examples.
apply_10min: Run the 90-line hybrid_retrieve() pipeline against your OKF corpus; log which stage contributes each final hit.

reference_projects.name: Neo4j GDS
reference_projects.description: Graph Data Science library for advanced traversal + community algorithms.

consulting_angle: Sell '4-stage retrieval upgrade' with before/after via the Nugget-14 rubric — 4–6 week deliverable, high measurable ROI.
twin_platform_hook: Expose as single MCP tool okf_hybrid_retrieve; supervisor calls it, specialists get pre-ranked context. Retrieval-as-a-service inside the platform.
